title: We really should spread the word about the aliens.

transcript.text: So, this video is what the fact that aliens are flying around our planet watching us means to us.
I remember I mentioned about aliens to one of my friends and he goes,
What does that mean to me? What does that mean to my life?
I don't see aliens, you know. They might as well not be here, you know.
If they're here, why don't they just land?
Well, I have two answers to that. One is what does it mean to you is that, well, they're here.
And if you believe that they're here, then they'll, if everybody believes that they're here, then they'll be able to land.
And if you don't believe that they're here, they can't land.
It's kind of like a bunch of people being in a room suffocating to death.
And the only way to get out of that room is to open up a door.
But people not noticing that that's a door.
People going, oh, that's just a square shape over there with a golden knob coming off of it.
And, you know, an individual, people going up to the door and trying to open up the knob, but it's locked, you know.
And the only way to open up the door is if everybody grabs the knob together and opens it.
And once they open up the door, all the oxygen can come in and all the help can come in.
But you have to open up the door.
So that's why.
That's what it means to you is that you have to acknowledge it.
You have to admit it.
You have to share that knowledge with other people.
And then that's what will allow them to come down.
Because that's what it means to you.
And it's true.
If you share your knowledge with other people, then they will believe.
It doesn't matter how close-minded they are.
If you share it with them and you tell them enough times, they're going to believe.
Because all you need to do is know.
The only problem is you have to kind of do it on your own.
And you can't expect the government to tell you.
Because as we all know, you know, our public schools don't have Aliens 101, Aliens 102.
It's not even elective.
You know, much less a requirement.
It's not even elective.
And so, you know, you've got to do it on your own.
And just because it's not in the school system doesn't mean that it's not there.
So, and it doesn't really take that much time.
Sometimes people go, oh, you know, I don't have time in my life to look at this kind of stuff.
To watch these videos on YouTube and to hear these interviews.
You know, I've got stuff to do.
I've got to watch The Sopranos.
You know, I've got to see what's going on in a new episode of The Hill or whatever.
And, you know, all my answer to that is, you know, just make time for it.
It's interesting stuff.
If you believe it, then it'll be true for you.
It's easy to watch.
And, you know, you don't have to watch TV.
You don't have to go to a bar and drink beer and listen to music.
You know, you can make a little bit of time for some other things in your life.
But, you know, as far as the psychology, a little introduction to the psychology of it is, you know,
We don't have to have, like, everybody to grab the knob and open it together.
Because other people, people, psychologically, generally speaking,
Basically will believe what they're told.
They will believe what the status quo, what the consensus says.
Obviously, nobody believes that aliens are here because people don't believe that aliens are here.
You're not told that they're here in school, so then you don't believe that they're here, right?
If you were told that they're here in school, well, obviously, you would believe that they're here.
So, you only need, like, groups of people.
If you get groups of people to believe, then the people around them will believe.
It's kind of like when you're cooking free-based cocaine.
You mix the cocaine with the baking powder, and you have that little mixture of milk there.
And you're boiling it, and you're cooking it with a lighter from below.
And you put your paper clip in, and you stir the paper clip around.
You know, the paper clip will collect some globules.
And then you stem it around, and it'll just collect and collect, and then it'll grow, grow, grow big, big, big.
And next thing you know, there's no crack, free-flowing crack.
It's just water.
And then you have this big, you know, crystal of crack right there.
So, it's kind of like that.
And especially if you get pockets of people.

Well, you know, I just told him the stories.
And I told him everything I knew.

And every time we go out, he's like, ooh, where's the aliens?
Oh, where's the aliens?
